question:
Suppose you want to estimate $\boldsymbol{\mu}_1-\boldsymbol{\mu}_2$ with $95 \%$ confidence for a problem similar to the cholesterol illustrative example. You want your margin of error to be no greater than $0.25 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$. 
(a)We have established that $s^2{ }_{\text {pooled }}=0.24813$, and will use this as a reasonable estimate of $\sigma^2$.
（b）Now suppose that you can recruit only 20 individuals into group 1 . How many individuals will you now need in group 2?
answer:
(a)For 95% confidence, use $z_{1-(0.05 / 2)}=1.96$. The sample size required per group is $n=\frac{2 \sigma^2 z^2}{m^2}=\frac{2 \cdot 0.24813 \cdot 1.96^2}{0.25^2}=30.5$. Round this up to the next integer and resolve to use 31 individuals per group.
(b) $n_2=\frac{n n_1}{2 n_1-n}=\frac{31 \cdot 20}{2 \cdot 20-31}=68.9$. Round this up to 69 .

meta info.caption: Sample size requirement for a confidence interval for $\mu_1-\mu_2$
